description:
You are an “artifact-manager” in charge of curating a small collection of binary
    artefacts.  
    Everything you do must happen in your normal account under /home/user.  
    Your goal is to create and populate a brand-new SQLite database, then export two text
    artefacts that will be verified by an automated test.

    0. Directory layout  
       • Work only inside /home/user/artifacts           ← must be created by you  
       • All files referenced below have to live in that directory.

    1. Database creation  
       • File name: /home/user/artifacts/repository.db  
       • The database must contain precisely the two tables (and one index) shown
         below.  The identifiers (table names, column names, index name) are case-sensitive
         and must match exactly.

         Table 1: binaries  
           id           INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
           name         TEXT    NOT NULL  
           version      TEXT    NOT NULL  
           sha256       TEXT    NOT NULL UNIQUE  
           size_kb      INTEGER NOT NULL  
           uploaded_at  TEXT    NOT NULL        -- ISO-8601 UTC, keep the “Z” suffix

         Table 2: tags  
           id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
           binary_id  INTEGER NOT NULL          -- FK => binaries(id)  
           tag        TEXT    NOT NULL

         Index: idx_binaries_sha256 on binaries(sha256)

    2. Data that must be inserted  
       Insert exactly the following three binaries and five tags.  
       (The id columns should be left to SQLite’s AUTOINCREMENT behaviour.)

       === binaries table ===
       name       | version | sha256                                                               | size_kb | uploaded_at
       -----------+---------+-----------------------------------------------------------------------+---------+--------------------------
       compressor | 1.2.0   | 3a7bd3e2360af45d8c0b1b1b35d4c32789f3e8ac1f6b8572e9a5f4e5d4bc3a       |  5120   | 2024-03-01T10:30:00Z
       linker     | 2.0.1   | 59d1ab0dfe3cc14d2bf3a1a9cc5a6c70e2f0e5d7b3e6a4f8e0c9b1d2e5f7a8b       |  3072   | 2024-03-02T11:00:00Z
       packager   | 0.9.5   | a794b8f5de60fa3ea4c2a9d87e9c9f1f3d2e1c4b5a6d7c8e9f0b1a2c3d4e5f        |  8192   | 2024-03-03T09:15:00Z

       === tags table ===
       (binary names are shown only for clarity—you must use the correct foreign keys)
       compressor → “stable”
       compressor → “cli”
       linker     → “stable”
       packager   → “experimental”
       packager   → “cli”

    3. Text artefact #1 – joint report  
       • Path: /home/user/artifacts/binary_tag_report.txt  
       • Create it using an SQLite query that joins binaries and tags.  
       • Each logical record must be rendered on one single line using the exact pipe
         separator ‘|’ in the following order of fields:

           name|version|sha256|tag

       • Records must be sorted alphabetically by “name”, then by “tag”.  
       • The file must contain exactly five lines (one per tag assignment) and end with
         a single trailing newline.  No header line.

       Example of the expected format (values will be checked for correctness):

         compressor|1.2.0|<sha256>|cli  
         compressor|1.2.0|<sha256>|stable  
         … etc …

    4. Text artefact #2 – schema dump  
       • Path: /home/user/artifacts/repository_schema.sql  
       • Use the SQLite “.schema” meta-command to generate it.  
       • The file must contain the CREATE statements for both tables AND the CREATE
         INDEX statement, nothing else.  Preserve the exact identifier spellings.

    5. Allowable actions  
       The whole job can be achieved with roughly 5–10 shell or sqlite3 commands, e.g.:
       ‑ mkdir, sqlite3, and a couple of SQL/\.commands.  Use any approach you like
       as long as the final state matches the requirements above.

    6. Verification notes  
       Automated tests will:
       • Check that /home/user/artifacts/repository.db exists and is a valid SQLite DB.  
       • Verify the tables, column names, and index via PRAGMA statements.  
       • Select * from binaries and tags to match the exact rows given.  
       • Confirm that binary_tag_report.txt matches the required content and order.  
       • Run “sqlite3 repository.db .schema” and compare with repository_schema.sql.

    Do NOT hard-code absolute IDs when inserting; let SQLite assign them.
truth:
compressor|1.2.0|3a7bd3e2360af45d8c0b1b1b35d4c32789f3e8ac1f6b8572e9a5f4e5d4bc3a|cli compressor|1.2.0|3a7bd3e2360af45d8c0b1b1b35d4c32789f3e8ac1f6b8572e9a5f4e5d4bc3a|stable linker|2.0.1|59d1ab0dfe3cc14d2bf3a1a9cc5a6c70e2f0e5d7b3e6a4f8e0c9b1d2e5f7a8b|stable packager|0.9.5|a794b8f5de60fa3ea4c2a9d87e9c9f1f3d2e1c4b5a6d7c8e9f0b1a2c3d4e5f|cli packager|0.9.5|a794b8f5de60fa3ea4c2a9d87e9c9f1f3d2e1c4b5a6d7c8e9f0b1a2c3d4e5f|experimental CREATE TABLE binaries( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, version TEXT NOT NULL, sha256 TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE, size_kb INTEGER NOT NULL, uploaded_at TEXT NOT NULL ); CREATE TABLE tags( id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, binary_id INTEGER NOT NULL, tag TEXT NOT NULL, FOREIGN KEY(binary_id) REFERENCES binaries(id) ); CREATE INDEX idx_binaries_sha256 ON binaries(sha256); compressor 1.2.0 3a7bd3e2360af45d8c0b1b1b35d4c32789f3e8ac1f6b8572e9a5f4e5d4bc3a 5120 2024-03-01T10:30:00Z linker 2.0.1 59d1ab0dfe3cc14d2bf3a1a9cc5a6c70e2f0e5d7b3e6a4f8e0c9b1d2e5f7a8b 3072 2024-03-02T11:00:00Z packager 0.9.5 a794b8f5de60fa3ea4c2a9d87e9c9f1f3d2e1c4b5a6d7c8e9f0b1a2c3d4e5f 8192 2024-03-03T09:15:00Z
